text:
Once upon a time in a small town nestled between mountains and woods, there lived an old carpenter named Elias. He was known throughout the village for his delicate woodwork and his cheerful voice singing hymns. Despite his age, he worked tirelessly, building chairs, chests of drawers, and shelves that lined the quaint little shops around town.

Elias had a granddaughter named Lily. She was adventurous and always eager to learn new things. Despite her busy schedule at the village school, she loved spending time with her grandfather. One afternoon, after finishing his chores, Elias suggested they have a picnic under the big oak tree in the park.

Lily brought along some sandwiches and bananas. They ate under the old tree, surrounded by babblers and buzzing bees. Elias talked about old times and how they used to hunt together way back when.

Suddenly, a sharp cry cut through the tranquillity. Elias looked toward a tall cliff nearby. There was a fox! It had seen Lily. With quick thinking
